diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers reached the same unsupported label and used no citations, so there is no meaningful citation difference. The main difference is how they coded the lack of support: option 1 uses the more claim-specific code central_proposition_unsupported, while option 2 uses the broader code insufficient_evidence. Their rationales are substantively aligned rather than conflicting: both say the evidence does not support skipping the rollback plan, with option 1 adding a more pointed reference to evidence item 3 showing a rollback plan exists and helped unblock a release. Because the labels, uncited evidence use, and confidence are the same, the disagreement is best explained as reason-code granularity rather than evidence interpretation.
